name: Peñíscola Marshland Trail
name_original: Peñíscola — Circular por el Castillo y La Marjal
description: Easy 3.4-mile hike in Peñíscola, combining the historic castle with the tranquil wetlands of La Marjal, taking 1h 25m.
region_intro: Hiking around Marjal de Peñiscola is characterized by a distinctive coastal wetland ecosystem, featuring lagoons, canals, and marshy terrain. The area is defined by "ullals," which are groundwater springs, and artificial canals like the Riu Ample, aiding drainage towards the sea. This landscape supports diverse marsh and saltmarsh vegetation, offering accessible paths through a protected natural environment. Best bus station hiking trails around Marjal de Peñiscola The most popular bus station hiking route is Peñíscola Marshland Trail, an easy 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 25 minutes to complete. This route immerses hikers in the tranquil wetlands, allowing observation of lagoons and canals. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ullal de l'Estany – Peñíscola sign loop from Peníscola, an easy 3.9 miles (6.3 km) path. This trail offers a straightforward walk through the local surroundings, highlighting the wetland's unique features. Local hikers also love the Peñíscola sign – Porta de Sant Pere loop from Marjal de Peníscola, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail leading through marshy terrain and canals, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Hiking around Marjal de Peñiscola is defined by coastal wetland ecosystems, serene lagoons, and a network of canals, offering accessible paths for various ability levels. The region provides 8 easy, 5 moderate, and 1 difficult route. The routes in Marjal de Peñiscola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 4,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Marjal de Peñiscola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many bus station hiking trails are available in Marjal de Peñíscola?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes in the Marjal de Peñíscola area that are easily accessible from bus stations, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on these bus-accessible trails?
A: The trails accessible from bus stations in Marjal de Peñíscola primarily traverse a unique coastal wetland ecosystem. You'll encounter serene lagoons, canals (like the Riu Ample), and wet meadows. Many routes also offer views of the historic Peñíscola Castle, blending natural beauty with cultural heritage.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes accessible by bus?
A: Yes, the Marjal de Peñíscola offers several easy paths suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e878721090/peniscola-marshland-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñíscola Marshland Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.5 km route that immerses you in the tranquil wetlands, perfect for a leisurely family stroll.

Q: Can I bring my dog on these bus station hiking trails?
A: Many trails in the Marjal de Peñíscola are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural surroundings with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect the delicate wetland ecosystem and its wildlife. Always check local regulations for specific routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Marjal de Peñíscola?
A: While Peñíscola is known for its sunny summers, the Marjal is best explored during &lt;strong&gt;autumn, winter, and spring&lt;/strong&gt;. These seasons offer mild weather perfect for walking, and in spring, the landscape becomes vibrant with color and bird activity.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I spot on the trails?
A: The Marjal de Peñíscola is a haven for wildlife. It's an important birdwatching site, home to species like the grey heron, purple swamphen, and kingfisher. You might also spot rare endemic fish species like the samaruc and fartet in the 'ullals' (groundwater springs) and canals.

Q: Are there any circular routes starting near the bus station?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38254146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullal de l'Estany – Peñíscola sign loop from Peníscola&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.3 km circular hike that allows you to experience the wetland's unique features and return to your starting point.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the Marjal de Peñíscola trails?
A: The trails in Marjal de Peñíscola are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the tranquil wetland scenery, the accessibility of the paths, and the rich biodiversity, especially for birdwatching.

Q: Are there any longer hikes accessible from the bus station?
A: For a longer experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40373070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñíscola sign – Porta de Sant Pere loop from Marjal de Peníscola&lt;/a&gt;. This easy 7.4 km route offers a more extended exploration of the wetland landscape, typically taking around 1 hour 54 minutes to complete.

Q: What are 'ullals' and can I see them on the trails?
A: The 'ullals' are a defining feature of the Marjal de Peñíscola landscape. They are groundwater springs that continuously feed the wetland. Many trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33864973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Marjal of Peñíscola – Ullal de l'Estany loop from Peníscola&lt;/a&gt;, will take you past these unique and ecologically important natural springs.

Q: Are there observation points or boardwalks along the bus-accessible routes?
A: Yes, the Marjal de Peñíscola features a long wooden boardwalk and several observation points designed to enhance your experience. These allow visitors to enjoy the diverse vegetation and abundant fauna without disturbing the ecosystem, often with interpretive panels explaining the local biodiversity.
tips:
One of the most beautiful and picturesque castles in the Valencian Community. Located on the rock, getting to its doors is a walk not to be missed, you will go through the narrow streets, the colorful houses and its panoramic views along the whole way. An ideal place to visit with the whole family.
Curious fact, it is an internationally recognized castle after some scenes of Game of Thrones were filmed there.

Very close to the town center is the Marjal, an underground wetland with small lakes on the surface known as ullals and are a source of surface water, very similar to those found in various places in the Valencian Community, for example in Swedish near La Albufera.

faqs.question: Is the Peñíscola — Circular by the Castle and La Marjal trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is an easy hike, suitable for all skill levels and great for families with children. The paths are easily accessible, making it a pleasant experience for everyone.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies, transitioning from the tranquil, marshy landscape of The Marjal of Peñíscola, characterized by serene lagoons and wet meadows, to the rocky promontory where Peñíscola Castle stands. Expect easily accessible paths throughout.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse scenery. You'll experience the ecological richness of The Marjal of Peñíscola with its wetlands and birdlife, and then the historical grandeur of Peñíscola Castle, offering stunning panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and the old town.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly recommended during autumn, winter, and spring. During these seasons, the weather is mild, and the landscape of The Marjal of Peñíscola is vibrant and full of life.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Peñíscola — Circular by the Castle and La Marjal trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally dog-friendly, it's important to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through The Marjal of Peñíscola, which is a protected natural area. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Marjal de Peñíscola?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits required or entrance fees to access the natural areas of The Marjal of Peñíscola or the trail itself. However, there may be an entrance fee if you wish to enter Peñíscola Castle.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Peñíscola offers various parking options, especially near the old town and the coastal areas. It's advisable to look for public parking lots in Peñíscola town, which provide convenient access to the starting points of the circular route.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of official trails such as El Camí IT6TR3 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, carrer de Jaume I, and Passatge Cap del Mar, among others.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I spot in The Marjal of Peñíscola?
faqs.answer: In The Marjal of Peñíscola, you can observe various bird species like the grey heron and bittern. It's also a refuge for unique species such as the samaruc fish, making it an ecological gem.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: While the natural sections of the trail through The Marjal of Peñíscola are undeveloped, once you reach the historic area around Peñíscola Castle and the old town, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and public restrooms.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given its easy difficulty, comfortable walking shoes, water, and sun protection (hat, sunscreen) are generally sufficient. If you plan to visit the castle, consider bringing some cash or a card for the entrance fee.
